reasoning: The forecast requires predicting whether the Indeed job posting index for software development will be above its April 27, 2025 value (63.56) on four future dates. The long-term macroeconomic trend over the last two years is unmistakably downward, with values dropping from ~69.6 in April 2024 to ~63.5 currently. Although there was a sharp recovery from an anomalous low in mid-March (61.64) to early April (64.08), the index has resumed a slight downward drift over the past 15 days, landing at 63.56. The FRED tool's statistical output of P=0.69 is structurally flawed for two reasons: (1) its linear model overfits the one-time bounce from the March anomaly, and (2) its seasonal module inappropriately compared last year's absolute May reading (68.93) to this year's threshold (63.56) without detrending for the year-over-year drop. Correcting for this, the underlying short- and long-term momentum is slightly negative. Therefore, probabilities of a near-term increase are below 0.5. As explicitly instructed, uncertainty strictly increases for more distant horizons, progressing linearly toward 0.50.
